name: GeGLU
oneliner: GLU variant using GELU activation instead of Swish — performs nearly identically to SwiGLU, used in T5 v1.1 and Flan-T5.
explanation: GeGLU is a gated activation function that uses the GELU nonlinearity as its gating mechanism, introduced by Shazeer in 2020. In quality benchmarks it is practically indistinguishable from SwiGLU, both significantly outperforming ReLU. The T5 and Flan-T5 model families adopted GeGLU, while the Llama-inspired ecosystem standardized on SwiGLU instead. Since GELU was already the standard activation in BERT and GPT-2, GeGLU was a natural extension. It has become less common in decoder-only LLMs since 2023.
fundamentals: GeGLU(x) = (GELU(xW_gate) ⊙ xW_up)W_down. GELU(z) $\approx$ z·0.5·(1 + tanh($\sqrt{2/π}$·(z + 0.044715$z^3$))). Same parameter count as SwiGLU: 3 $\times$ d_model $\times$ d_ff. Swish vs GELU: $\Phi$(x) has slightly steeper transition than $\sigma$(x), negligible practical difference.
